title: Review Card
public_badge: Shareable version; amounts, dates, tickers, and exact weights removed
public_custom_rule: One self-authored process rule was committed; its wording stays on the review card.
demo_badge: Demo data and rehearsal only; not your trades and not written to formal coach memory

sections.attribution: Return attribution · theme exposure or selection
sections.improve: Improve · preserve strengths, remove process loss

blocks.trades: Key trades · ranked by money impact

block_missing.absolute_pnl: Absolute P&L is not computable this period: no trades or valuation basis.
block_missing.annualized: Annualized return is not computable this period: the cash-flow anchor is incomplete.
block_missing.annualized_prices: Annualized return is not computable this period: current prices could not be retrieved, so no portfolio-level valuation exists.
block_missing.vs_market: Benchmark comparison is not computable this period: no resolvable benchmark series.
block_missing.vs_market_prices: Benchmark comparison is not computable this period: the benchmark prices could not be retrieved.
block_missing.trades: No ranked instrument diagnosis this period.
block_missing.trades_traded: No ranked instrument diagnosis this period; traded: {tickers}.
block_missing.risks: No behavioral diagnosis this period: the engine scored no dimension.
block_missing.rule: No new rule commitment this time; you can pick one at the next review.
block_missing.rule_standing: No rule change this period; the standing rule remains: "{rule}"
block_missing.rule_skip: You chose not to set a new commitment; the same baseline remains available next time.
block_missing.rule_snapshot: This opening check keeps the structural baseline without forcing a commitment.
block_missing.rule_structural: This is an opening structural check: it sets a baseline without forcing a commitment. A later file with complete buy-and-sell history unlocks the full behavioral review — exit discipline, holding period, and win/loss.
block_missing.rule_insufficient_data: The sample is still short, so this review sets a baseline without forcing a commitment.
block_missing.snapshot_unlock: Importing your transaction-history CSV unlocks behavior diagnostics (win rate, payoff ratio, averaging-down discipline, and more).

etf_classification.allocation: Diversified allocation ETFs were separated from single-name concentration: {list}.
etf_classification.concentrated: Sector, thematic, or leveraged ETFs remained concentration risk: {list}.

public_patterns.exit_discipline: The highlighted behavior concerned how exit decisions were timed and evaluated.
public_patterns.position_sizing: The highlighted behavior concerned how risk was distributed across position sizes.
public_patterns.diversification: The highlighted behavior concerned whether the portfolio represented distinct underlying risks.
public_patterns.holding_period: The highlighted behavior concerned whether holding durations matched a consistent decision horizon.
public_patterns.averaging_down: The highlighted behavior concerned how additions to losing positions were bounded.
public_patterns.alpha_beta: The highlighted behavior concerned how benchmark-relative results were attributed between exposure and selection.
public_patterns.entry_style: The highlighted behavior concerned when entries occurred relative to recent price moves.

rules.exit_discipline: Before exiting, name the fact that completed or broke the thesis. Fear of giving back gains is not enough.
rules.position_sizing: Cap any single position at {cap}. Trim if it goes over, and do not add.
rules.diversification: Before adding a position, check whether it is still the same underlying driver. If that bet is already too large, do not add.
rules.holding_period: Label the trade as short-term, swing, or long-term at entry. Exit only for a reason from the same horizon.
rules.averaging_down: Before averaging down, write one piece of evidence you did not know at entry. If you cannot, do not add.

rule_grounding.position_sizing: This period: your largest single position {ticker} is {pct} of the portfolio.
rule_grounding.diversification: This period: your top positions {tickers} together hold {pct}.
rule_grounding.holding_period: This period: {tickers} mixed quick exits and long holds within the same position.
rule_grounding.averaging_down: This period: you averaged down {count} times into losing positions, including {tickers}.

rule_targets.line: What this rule would catch this period: {items}

rule_tradeoff.sizing_vs_proven_edge: The cap limits what a single position can cost you; it does not contradict the stock-picking edge.
rule_tradeoff.sizing_vs_hypothesis: The cap limits what a single position can cost you, and the sector call is still unverified — not something to back with a bigger single bet.

patterns_panel.label: A pattern, not yet judged (no answer needed)
patterns_panel.sold_winner_early: {early} of the {total} positions you sold kept rising afterwards, most clearly {tickers}.

exit_consistency.question: Was that a consistent exit rule, an emotional reaction, or an external constraint?

add_choices.price_only: Only the price fell / lower the cost basis

reduce.price_target: The planned reduction point was reached
reduce.anxiety: Locked in part of the gain

honesty.snapshot_scope: Transaction-history dimensions — averaging down, exit discipline, holding behavior, win rate, payoff, alpha, and historical motives — are out of scope for this position-snapshot review.
honesty.alpha_credibility: The alpha sample or statistical strength is insufficient; it is not evidence of durable skill.
honesty.sector_attribution: Some positions lack sector benchmarks, so allocation-versus-selection attribution is incomplete.
honesty.unclassified_drivers: Some positions are unclassified, so portfolio concentration may be understated.
honesty.price_source: Current prices did not come from the engine's own retrieval this period; they were either supplied from a named external source and date, or unavailable, and the card says which.
honesty.price_plausibility: A supplied closing price differs sharply from that ticker's own last recorded trade price; the source should be re-verified before the figure is trusted.
honesty.unrealized_coverage: Some positions lack current prices, so unrealized P&L is incomplete.
honesty.orphan_sells: Some exits lack a known entry; their realized P&L was excluded.
honesty.currency_mix: The portfolio spans currencies; aggregate figures use a common currency and some FX may be approximate.
honesty.cash_reliability: Cash lacks complete anchors, limiting account-level interpretation.
honesty.acct_perf_basis: Account performance uses partial cost or FX approximations and remains uncertain.
honesty.accounting_reconciliation: The imported transactions did not reproduce the snapshot-anchored current holdings, so current sizing, diversification, and unrealized P&L were not scored.
honesty.snapshot_reconciliation: This declared snapshot was compared with the ledger-derived holdings; a match was marked reconciled, and any difference was recorded as an explicit adjustment with the newer declaration as the accounting anchor, without guessing why the numbers differ.
honesty.etf_metadata: ETF expense-ratio or tracking-error data is incomplete; missing values were not treated as zero.
honesty.prior_commitment_breach: This period already recorded a breach of that same rule, before this review closed the books on it.

instrument_tags.suspected_averaging_down_losing: likely averaging a loser: {n_adds} adds only while losing, now down {cur_abs_pct}{price_note} (confirm the thesis)
instrument_tags.suspected_averaging_down_recovered: likely averaged a loser, now green: {n_adds} adds only while losing — the recovery looks like luck, not discipline
instrument_tags.adds_pending_confirmation: {n_adds} adds to confirm: planned DCA or averaging a loser — your call
instrument_tags.suspected_dca: likely DCA: {n_adds} buys up and down, not averaging a loser
instrument_tags.deep_underwater: down {cur_abs_pct}{price_note} and still held without a decision

stress_test.line: Your exposure to {label} is about {exposure} ({pct} of the portfolio); a 30% drawdown would mean −{drop30} on paper, a 50% drawdown −{drop50} — could you sit through that?

prescription_kinds.selection_inconclusive: Stock picking: not enough data to judge

prescription_texts.amplify_hypothesis: Of your {excess_pp}pp lead over the benchmark, {alloc_pp}pp came from being in the right theme. That is still a hypothesis rather than proven skill — a right call on AI can simply be standing in the wind, and only the next few calls can tell.
prescription_texts.selection_inconclusive: Within sectors, stock selection contributed {sel_pp}pp (descriptive; that number stands). But {t_note}, so the data cannot yet separate skill from luck.
prescription_texts.selection_inconclusive_t_wide: the alpha 95% interval is still too wide (t={t})
prescription_texts.selection_inconclusive_t_unstable: the alpha statistic is still unstable
prescription_texts.outsource_selection: Net of theme exposure, your in-sector stock selection contributed {sel_pp}pp and the shortfall is statistically solid. That does not mean giving up stock picking — part of the money can sit in a passive base with stock picks as the satellite. (A process observation, not a ticker recommendation.)
prescription_texts.amplify_selection_edge: Net of theme tailwinds, your in-sector stock selection still contributed {sel_pp}pp with statistically significant alpha — a real edge.
prescription_texts.cut_averaging_down: Adding to losing positions {count} times is the biggest pure leak in this record — the first behavior to remove.
prescription_texts.cut_oversize: Your largest position {ticker} holds {max_pct_pct} of the portfolio — a single oversized bet.

display.total: Total P&L was {total}: {realized} realized and {unrealized} unrealized.
display.realized_only: Realized P&L was {realized}; current unrealized P&L was not scored.
display.unrealized_only: Unrealized P&L was {unrealized}; realized P&L was unavailable.

alpha_interval.negative_caveat: (The interval includes a negative value, meaning this period's stock-picking edge is not yet statistically confirmed as a durable skill.)
alpha_interval.lead: Risk-adjusted alpha{scope} was {alpha}% annualized, with a 95% interval from {low}% to {high}%; the interval controls how strong the conclusion may be.
alpha_interval.compact: A 95% interval ran from {low}% to {high}%; the interval controls how strong the conclusion may be.

best_strength.no_signal: No positive behavior was strong enough to claim; keep attention on the largest leak.

reconciliation.statement_with_metric: Last time you committed: "{rule}" — the tracked number was {then} then, {now} now.

overview.opening: This is an opening portfolio check of {subject}.
overview.opening_as_of: This is an opening portfolio check of {subject} as of {as_of}.
overview.valuation_market_value: Structural weights use the supplied market-value basis.
overview.valuation_cost: Structural weights use the supplied cost basis.
overview.valuation_unavailable: No reliable valuation basis was available, so weight-based structure remains unscored.
overview.missing_fx: Reliable FX coverage was missing for: {currencies}.

strength.complete: The supplied snapshot establishes a complete structural baseline for the opening portfolio check.
strength.partial: The available fields establish a structural baseline; missing inputs remain explicit rather than inferred.
strength.baseline: The supplied positions establish a structural baseline; weight-based strengths remain unscored.

holes.leading_risk: The leading structural risk in the available snapshot was {label}.
holes.clean_structure: This position snapshot did not flag concentration or diversification as a structural risk.
holes.no_weights: This opening check establishes a structural baseline without treating unavailable weights as low risk.
